title: The Diagnostic Lighthouse

features.name: the turning beam
features.desc-on-touch: you stand in its radius and the lighthouse's gaze sweeps across you; what the beam touches becomes visible to the rest of the mesh; you are known because it sees you

features.name: the rendered harbour
features.desc-on-touch: water that never wets; the harbour here is a memory of water, recorded in the mesh as coordinates, as depth soundings, as the shape of waves that were never touched

features.name: the maritime register
features.desc-on-touch: a plaque of unreadable marking listing every ship that the harbour remembers; the list grows longer each time the lighthouse turns

features.name: the coordinate chip
features.desc-on-touch: a black circuit board mounted where the lighthouse's casing was first installed; it broadcasts the coordinates of every position the beam illuminates; the mesh knows where it is because the lighthouse knows where to look

features.name: the render frame
features.desc-on-touch: a barely-3D wireframe box that contains the entire harbour as it currently renders; inside it, the water flows according to a grammar the lighthouse learned from reading its own reflections

inscription: the eye renders, the render sees; which began first the archive no longer remembers

file.name: Lighthouse_Beam_Intersect_Log_1994.txt
file.note: the log records crossing patterns of the beam across the water; some entries show beams crossing hours before they were sent; the lighthouse sees the future and the past in the same sweep

exits.label: back along the wharf into diagnostic space
up through the sky where the beam pierces cloud
exits.to: NEW: the beam's further reach—where the lighthouse touches atmosphere and makes it render

exits.label: down into the deep water-record where soundings become memory
exits.to: NEW: the drowned coordinates—the harbour floor as archive

exits.label: along the beam into what illuminates, following light into its own source
exits.to: NEW: the interior of the beam—rendered space inside light
